document.title: Risk Management Report for G Series

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer risk management report for H&abyz G Series digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management documentation for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector under listed EU and Korea regulations and relevant medical device standards.
keyPoints: The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
The document lists EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea as applicable national and regional regulations.
EN ISO 14971:2019 is used for risk management, with risk acceptability divided into acceptable levels 1-4 and unacceptable levels 5-25.
After risk control measures, all 49 identified residual risks are reported as acceptable with 0 unacceptable residual risks.

Production and post-production information is collected through customer surveys, complaints, inspection monitoring, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and supplier assessment monitoring.

evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev. 0 established on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Oct/18/2024 Revision Description Establishment

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by R&D team Kim, Myeong seop Oct 18, 2024 Reviewed by R&D Manager Lee, Dong ho Oct 18, 2024 Approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Oct 18, 2024
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Report for G Series

evidence.claim: The device classification stated in the report is IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector classification

evidence.claim: The expected service life stated in the report is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector service life

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The detector is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide real-time case diagnosis and treatment planning support.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infant and pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: The device supports wired or wireless digital radiography detector use and auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors... The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector technology

evidence.claim: The detector consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics that convert read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital flat panel detector technology

evidence.claim: The applicable national and regional regulations listed are EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017... Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea
evidence.pageTarget: Applicable medical device regulations

evidence.claim: The report identifies EN ISO 14971:2019 as a relevant standard for application of risk management to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: The risk analysis process includes intended use and misuse, safety-related characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, and risk estimation.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk analysis process consists of the following steps... description of the intended use... identification of the characteristics... identification of hazards and hazardous situations... estimation of risks for each hazardous situation
evidence.pageTarget: Risk analysis process

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability is defined as acceptable risk levels 1-4 and unacceptable risk levels 5-25.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25
evidence.pageTarget: Risk acceptability criteria

evidence.claim: Before risk control, the table reports 2 acceptable risks and 47 unacceptable risks, total 49.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results before risk control... EN ISO 14971:2019 2 47 49
evidence.pageTarget: Risk evaluation before risk control

evidence.claim: After risk control, the table reports 49 acceptable residual risks and 0 unacceptable residual risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results after risk control (Numbers in the regions) Classification Acceptable (AR) Unacceptable (UA) Total... EN ISO 14971:2019 49 0 49
evidence.pageTarget: Residual risk evaluation

evidence.claim: The report concludes all identified residual risks after implementation of risk controls are acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.
evidence.pageTarget: Residual risk evaluation

evidence.claim: Risk control measures included labeling, user manual, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, differentiated cable types, IEC 62304 software development process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.
evidence.pageTarget: Risk control measures

evidence.claim: The product contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.19 Does the medical device contain software? A The product contains software.
evidence.pageTarget: Medical device software

evidence.claim: The detector can connect to a PC via Wi-Fi.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector can be connected to a PC via Wi-Fi.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector connectivity

evidence.claim: The product does not store data according to the report.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.21 Does the medical device store data critical to patient care? N/A This product does not store data.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector data storage

evidence.claim: Routine cleaning is required using a soft, dry cloth after product use.
evidence.evidenceText: The G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP is required to be routinely cleaned using soft, dry cloth after using the product.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector cleaning

evidence.claim: The operating environment is 10°C to 35°C, 20% to 75% relative humidity, and 700 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating Environment - Temperature: 10℃ to 35℃ - Humidity: 20 % to 75 % relative humidity (non-condensing) - Air Pressure: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector environmental conditions

evidence.claim: The storage and transit environment is -20°C to 55°C, 10% to 95% relative humidity, and 500 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage and Transit Environment - Temperature: -20 ℃ to 55 ℃ - Humidity: 10 % to 95% relative humidity (non-condensing) - Air Pressure: 500 hPa to 1060 hPa
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector environmental conditions

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction surveys every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Customer satisfaction survey Conduct the activities such as maintenance and repair at customer site, once every 12 months in accordance with the feedback procedure
evidence.pageTarget: Production and post-production information

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting and reviewing production and post-production information and managing quality problems.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase
evidence.pageTarget: Production and post-production information

evidence.claim: Safety information and side effects are to be reported to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).
evidence.pageTarget: Adverse event reporting

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not mention FDA.
The document appears to be a manufacturer risk management report, not an authority-issued regulatory document.
Risk count evidence is conflicting: one section states 43 risks were identified, while risk evaluation tables total 49 risks.
Some extracted text contains apparent OCR or source transcription issues, including inconsistent standard references such as EN 15223-1 versus EN 15322-1.
